mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate reduces the discount rate applied to future corporate earnings, directly compressing equity valuation multiples and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A rapid increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.3 percentage points within a three-month window signals deteriorating labor market conditions, triggering risk-off positioning and negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5-day returns in more than 60% of triggered instances despite the unemployment spike, or the signal shows no predictive power in walk-forward tests.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent declines in real personal savings (RSAFS) and housing starts (HOUST) indicate a synchronized withdrawal of household demand, which reduces corporate revenue growth expectations and drives negative S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict negative returns when RSAFS and HOUST decline simultaneously, or single-proxy models outperform the interaction term in out-of-sample R-squared.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production growth (INDPRO) acts as a leading indicator for corporate capacity utilization and margin expansion, but only when the economy operates above potential output. Positive INDPRO surprises generate positive S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: INDPRO prints show no correlation with 20-day forward returns during late-cycle expansions, or the signal flips to negative predictive power when capacity utilization exceeds 85%.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly real GDP growth (GDPC1) directly reflects aggregate economic momentum. Accelerating GDP growth improves near-term earnings revisions, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward returns show no statistical dependence on GDP growth acceleration, or the signal produces negative returns during confirmed GDP expansion phases.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate exceeds 4.5 percent, borrowing costs suppress corporate investment and consumer credit demand, leading to compressed earnings multiples and negative S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: Equity markets rally or remain flat when the federal funds rate sustains levels above 4.5 percent for consecutive quarters, contradicting the discount rate compression thesis.

mechanism_narrative: Rising nonfarm payrolls (PAYEMS) combined with a stable or falling unemployment rate (UNRATE) confirms robust labor demand without wage-push inflation fears, supporting equity valuations and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint PAYEMS and UNRATE signal fails to predict positive returns when both metrics improve simultaneously, or the interaction term adds no incremental predictive power over single-proxy models.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts (HOUST) serve as a high-frequency proxy for consumer confidence and construction sector health. In post-shock recovery environments, rising housing activity accelerates wealth effects and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over 5 trading days.
failure_condition: HOUST prints show no positive correlation with 5-day forward returns during post-shock recoveries, or the signal turns negative when housing activity rises alongside rising inflation expectations.
